reason_deleted: The one-shot worker-handoff bookmark review had already been performed and recorded; leaving it active could create unnecessary side-session interruption.

schedule_assessment.ec2_cost_sentinel_hourly: correct; GPU spend safety needs frequent independent checks
schedule_assessment.two_hour_supervisor: correct; primary progress and anti-loop watchdog
schedule_assessment.cursor_worker_monitor_two_hour: correct; monitors usage-reduction lane without becoming project manager
schedule_assessment.claude_subscription_monitor_four_hour: correct; lower cadence is enough for high-effort synthesis lane health
schedule_assessment.six_hour_milestone_auditor: correct; sequence drift should be checked less often than progress
schedule_assessment.fleet_health_daily: correct; verify automation health and stale finalization without steering routine work

strategy_assessment.cursor_role: mechanical scans, evidence extraction, validator triage, path/hash summaries, first-pass drafts
strategy_assessment.claude_role: high-effort synthesis, contradiction review, architecture/routing critique
strategy_assessment.codex_role: final authority, live runtime decisions, visual QA, Git/AWS/S3/Jira/mask decisions, validation

remaining_watch_items: Let the combined Cursor/Claude monitor run for 2-3 cycles to prove estimated usage reduction.
Let the Claude subscription monitor produce its first scheduled audit at its next cadence.
Fleet health should classify superseded stale IN_PROGRESS stubs without creating cleanup churn.
If CODEX_USAGE_DRIFT appears, steer by delegating the next suitable task rather than interrupting with bookkeeping.
next_recommended_action: Keep the nine active cron jobs and one paused legacy heartbeat as-is; continue selected-inpaint ComfyUI runtime/orchestration work while worker monitors measure actual Codex usage reduction.
